title: Atypical chest discomfort documentation

red_flags.text: Supports documenting pain timing and characteristics, associated symptoms, cardiovascular history and risk, vital signs and examination, and clinician-reviewed ECG or test context; urgent conclusions remain clinician-led.
red_flags.rationale: The NICE section supports documenting chest-discomfort timing, characteristics, associated symptoms, cardiovascular history and risk, examination, and already reviewed ECG or tests without inferring an acute coronary syndrome.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting pain timing and characteristics, associated symptoms, cardiovascular history and risk, vital signs and examination, and clinician-reviewed ECG or test context; urgent conclusions remain clinician-led.

source.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis

exact_section.heading: Section 1.2 - acute chest-pain history, associated features, examination, ECG, and test context

investigations.text: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.
investigations.rationale: The information section supports recording uncertainty, patient questions, reviewed-test explanation, clinician-provided safety-netting, and follow-up without generating advice.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

source_limitations: Current symptom status, exact location and character, radiation, triggers, duration, associated symptoms, risk factors, prior disease and tests, vital signs, examination, ECG, biomarkers, diagnosis, urgency, treatment, referral, and safety-net content remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
